LanguageData.VVE_Lightning_Blueprint.description: A late-model Corvega supercar tuned for absurd road speed and almost nothing else. It devours gasoline, hates rough ground, and exists mainly to outrun whatever is behind you. As with most wasteland prestige cars, the speed comes at the cost of fuel economy, durability, and cargo room. <color=#bb8f04>Crew:</color> Driver x1, Passenger x1 <color=#bb8f04>Fuel type:</color> Gasoline
LanguageData.VVE_Lightning_Blueprint.label: Corvega lightning
LanguageData.VVE_Highwayman_Blueprint.description: A rugged Corvega APC rebuilt into an armed road escort. It trades comfort and efficiency for protection, firepower, and reliable highway travel. It still burns through gasoline at an alarming rate, but wasteland caravans keep them running because they are cheap, fast, and mean enough to scare off light raiders. <color=#bb8f04>Crew:</color> Driver x1, Gunner x1, Passenger x1 <color=#bb8f04>Fuel type:</color> Gasoline
LanguageData.VVE_Highwayman_Blueprint.label: Corvega APC
LanguageData.VVE_Prowler_Blueprint.description: A stripped-down Corvega scout buggy built for rough country, bad roads, and quick solo runs beyond the safety of settled routes. It is cheap, light, and useful in broken terrain, but its exposed frame makes it a poor choice for any real firefight. <color=#bb8f04>Crew:</color> Driver x1 <color=#bb8f04>Fuel type:</color> Gasoline
LanguageData.VVE_Prowler_Blueprint.label: Corvega prowler
LanguageData.VVE_Roadkill_Blueprint.description: A heavily armored Corvega road tank built around brute force, frontal plating, and simple wasteland survivability. It is slow, thirsty, and miserable to maintain, but caravans keep one around when they expect the highway to fight back. <color=#bb8f04>Crew:</color> Driver x1 <color=#bb8f04>Fuel type:</color> Gasoline
LanguageData.VVE_Roadkill_Blueprint.label: Corvega roadkill
LanguageData.VVE_Roadrunner_Blueprint.description: A larger Corvega cargo wagon meant to move serious loads without giving up the speed and road manners of a pre-War automobile. Compared to older wasteland cars it is more efficient, less visible, and far better suited to long-haul freight runs on maintained roads. <color=#bb8f04>Crew:</color> Driver x1, Passenger x1 <color=#bb8f04>Fuel type:</color> Gasoline
LanguageData.VVE_Roadrunner_Blueprint.label: Corvega roadrunner
LanguageData.VVE_Traveller_Blueprint.description: A lightweight pre-War Chryslus road car built for fast travel, simple cargo hauling, and staying on open pavement whenever possible. Like most surviving wasteland automobiles, it drinks gasoline greedily and performs worst when dragged into mud, hills, or winter terrain. <color=#bb8f04>Crew:</color> Driver x1, Passenger x1 <color=#bb8f04>Fuel type:</color> Gasoline
LanguageData.VVE_Traveller_Blueprint.label: chryslus highwayman
